title: Weyl Node To Berry Monopole
summary: A Weyl node can be read either as a linearized band crossing or as a momentum-space monopole of Berry curvature whose flux equals the node charge.
assumptions: The node is isolated and the occupied-state bundle is defined away from it.
regime: Local Weyl-node analysis with Berry geometry.
scope: Lecture One conceptual bridge between Hamiltonian and curvature pictures.

source_anchors.notes: The flux formula is the bridge between node and monopole viewpoints.

representation: Cross-perspective bridge from local Hamiltonian data to geometric flux.
failure_modes: The monopole language is only valid on the punctured region where the Berry bundle is defined.
retrieval_hints: Use when translating between band-crossing and Berry-curvature explanations.
